verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Almagreira After work (18:30 start)
verdict.reason: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Almagreira looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 16 kJ, 9 s period, lully 1/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 16 kJ, 9 s period, lully 2/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
Cerro may feel too soft without more push

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 20 kJ, 9 s period, lully 4/100, and wind is the main risk.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at mid tide stays weak and soft for Supertubos / Molhe Leste; Supertubos looks below your bodyboard fun size; SwellMap rating 1/10 Poor — caution.
ranked.shortExplanation: Supertubos is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is helping, but the spot still looks serious.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
